CONTEXT: Loads the current project context without editing files.
Reads the active state and workflow context with Tools/pipeline/read_compiled_context.ps1.
Selects matching knowledge notes with Tools/knowledge/context_tags.ps1 and Tools/knowledge/knowledge_select.ps1.
Loads programming context with Tools/codebase/load_programming_context.ps1 only when the requested context is code-touching.
Reports what context is loaded and the next executable step.
CONTINUE: Executes the next active pass read from _PLAN.json with Tools/json/json_read.ps1.
If no active pass remains, uses docs/TARGET.json as the prioritization lens.
Proposes the next target-aligned item from TODO, vision documents, or ideas instead of adding it without approval.
For code-touching work, loads programming context with Tools/codebase/load_programming_context.ps1 before source inspection.
After completing a pass, removes only that completed pass from _PLAN.json with JSON document scripts and finalizes through Tools/repo/done.ps1.
DO PASS N: Executes the explicitly requested pass read from _PLAN.json with Tools/json/json_read.ps1.
Treats the pass definition as the scope contract.
Refreshes the pass definition first with JSON document scripts if it is stale.
Preserves every unresolved future pass unless the user explicitly changes the plan, then finalizes through Tools/repo/done.ps1.
PROMPT: Defragments multiple prompts into _PROMPT-QUEUE.json with JSON document scripts, merges overlaps, sorts them, and clears _PROMPTS.md.
If _PROMPTS.md is empty, executes the next actionable queue pass read with Tools/json/json_read.ps1.
After successful execution, removes only the completed queue scope with JSON document scripts and finalizes through Tools/repo/done.ps1.
BUG: Selects the active bug with the best mix of current leverage, diagnostic readiness, reproduction likelihood, and future-change cost.
Applies the smallest useful diagnostic step or source-owned fix.
Asks the user to test before moving the bug to docs-tech/BUGS-SOLVED.json with JSON document scripts.
Reports the recommended next active bug when any remain and finalizes changed files through Tools/repo/done.ps1.
CONSOLE: Uses current Codex Run console output when available.
Stops if no console output exists.
Summarizes current Godot errors, warnings, and GDScript backtraces with Tools/gamedev/console_summary.py.
Fixes the highest-leverage source-owned runtime error.
Reports observed facts, likely cause, verification result, and remaining errors after finalizing through Tools/repo/done.ps1.
LOG <description>: Stops without inspecting or modifying files if the description is missing.
Finds the newest matching manual F4 dump and nearby similar captures from the same one-hour packet with Tools/gamedev/log_summary.py.
Uses paired screenshots and sidecar diagnostic tails when useful.
Applies the smallest source-owned fix for the described issue.
After explicit user confirmation, clears generated manual captures with top-level clear_logs.bat while preserving directory structure.
AUDIT: Runs the project document and process audit with Tools/documents/audit_docs.ps1.
Fixes local, unambiguous hard contract errors.
Reports soft warnings as recommendations unless cleanup was requested.
Keeps audit-driven changes limited to the directly affected documents or scripts and finalizes durable changes through Tools/repo/done.ps1.
COMPILE: Refreshes compiled SocratexAI instructions, project compiled context, and compiled knowledge outputs with Tools/pipeline/compile.ps1.
Checks whether compiled outputs are refreshed, stale, missing, or changed.
Stops when no committed source or generated file requires finalization.
Runs Tools/repo/done.ps1 only when compile changes committed files.

contract: This file contains only user-facing command semantics.
Do not store task flows here; put procedural task routing in FLOWS.json.
Do not store script catalog entries or script descriptions here; put those in SCRIPTS.json. Script references are allowed inside command behavior lines when they identify the tool used.
